summary: Backend regression after self-registration + Google auth refactor. Rewrote pytest suite (25 tests) — covers root, admin info, auth/verify (admin/unregistered/invalid sig), Google session error path, /auth/me cookie gate, /auth/logout, self-registration (sig path, mismatch rejection, bad-role rejection, idempotent update, missing-auth 401), Pinata real upload, records create + role gating, decrypt-key policy + grants, access request/respond/granted, LPA pending/preview/anchor/stats. All 25 tests pass.

minor.issue: Uses synchronous `requests` library inside async FastAPI handlers — blocks the event loop under load. Migrate to httpx.AsyncClient or run in a threadpool.
Accepts any doctor_address without signature verification — a malicious actor could spam pending requests in a patient's inbox. Approval is still safe (sig-gated), but the inbox vector is open.
File is 834 lines (>700 guideline). Consider splitting into routers/auth.py, routers/records.py, routers/lpa.py.

action_items: Optional: add signature verification on POST /api/access/request to prevent inbox spam.
Optional: switch Pinata + Google OAuth exchange calls from `requests` to `httpx.AsyncClient` to avoid blocking the event loop.
Optional refactor: split server.py (834 lines) into modular routers.
critical_code_review_comments: POST /api/access/request lacks signature verification — any caller can create pending requests for any doctor against any patient.
Synchronous `requests` calls inside async endpoints (google_session_exchange, ipfs_upload, ipfs_gateway) block the event loop.
server.py is 834 lines; should be split into routers per domain.
evaluate_policy() builds tokens twice (unused `tokens` regex on line 593) — dead code; remove.
Cookie set with samesite=none + secure=true is correct for cross-site preview, but requires HTTPS — confirmed via REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL.
test_credentials: Admin: 0x12606Fa5e40FAd4D99D59Ee967aF4c418b6E5D8B / 0xb61123baee2bb96625cfeabf70835eff22b81427cd030ec78e804d03d2dc7759 (deterministic from ADMIN_SEED). Test doctor/patient wallets are derived per-run with a uuid suffix.
seed_data_creation: Per-run unique TEST_doctor/TEST_patient/TEST_other_doc wallets are self-registered. Records, access grants, and LPA anchors are created during tests. Old TEST_* data persists in MongoDB across runs (no teardown) — acceptable for demo DB.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All backend endpoints are healthy (25/25 pytest). The registration model changed: use `actor_address`, `actor_message`, `actor_signature` (NO `address` field). Idempotent — re-POSTing same actor_address updates profile (no 409). Google session can only be tested for error path (401 on invalid session_id); end-to-end /auth/me + /auth/logout with a real session cannot be tested without a real Google OAuth handshake. Use `test_genc_backend.py` as regression baseline.
